What We Do

Five Pillars of NABS

📖

Learn

Rigorous Bible study beyond surface-level knowledge into doctrinal depth and textual understanding.

✝️

Grow

Scripture clubs, school outreach, and daily devotionals in Alimosho LGA and beyond.

🔍

Discover

We identify gifted young scholars — regardless of background or faith — and invest in their potential.

🌍

Impact

From sponsoring Pharmacy students to supporting Bible quiz champions — real, quiet, consistent impact.

👑

Lead

We produce scholars who carry divine authority — problem-solvers equipped with both intellect and the Word.

“Not just studying, but Doing the Word — this is not a tagline. It is a theology of action.”
— Stephen Olabamiji · Founder, National Assembly of Bible Scholars
The Evidence

Hamdalat’s Story

At our inaugural Lagos Bible Quiz in 2022, 33% of participants were Muslim students. The overall winner? Balogun Hamdalat — then an SS3 student, a Muslim girl who outscored every Christian participant through sheer dedication to Scripture study.

Today, Hamdalat is a 400-level university student. This is what NABS does: it makes the Word of God irresistible to every searching heart.

🏅

2022 Lagos Bible Quiz

Overall Champion

Balogun Hamdalat
Muslim Scholar · Now in 400L University
33% of quiz participants were Muslim — the Word of God transcends every boundary.

National Assembly of Bible Scholars — a movement built on the conviction that the Word of God, studied deeply and lived boldly, transforms every community it touches.

The Founder

Stephen Olabamiji

Economist · Data Analyst · Kingdom Builder · Founder of NABS, The 36 Innovative Delegates & Smallville Infotech

Stephen Olabamiji

Founder · NABS · Est. 2022 · Lagos

🏛 NABS

National Assembly of Bible Scholars — a movement of deep scripture engagement and community formation across Nigeria.

🌐 Smallville Infotech

The tech arm powering NABS’s digital infrastructure, including the NAIJA_NABS web application.

⚡ The 36 Innovative Delegates

A separate movement connecting innovation, leadership and purpose — indexed on Google Search Console.

His Story

A Lighthouse Built from Loss

At 27 years old, Stephen Adekanmbi Olabamiji is an Economics Graduate, Data Analyst, and what he quietly calls a “Kingdom Small-Scale Philanthropist” — though the scale of what he has built in silence far surpasses that modest description.

Stephen began fending for himself at age 17. When his mother passed, his three siblings came to live with him in Oke-Odo, Lagos. He became their father, provider, and spiritual anchor. Those four became the original four pillars of what would grow into NAIJA_NABS.

Before there were structures, there was a Sunday evening gathering: 40 children meeting around God’s Word in Alimosho Local Government Area. This was not a programme. This was a family in intercession, turning their lack into a lighthouse for others.

“I’ve made so many mistakes already — but God has always turned the experiences into mysteries that I’d later realise were blessings.”

— STEPHEN OLABAMIJI

In the past year, Stephen has been in active fellowship with seasoned US- and UN-trained personnel in FCT Abuja — core members of the prestigious Ashoka Fellowship. This season is drawing to a close, and God has declared: now is the time for NAIJA_NABS to take its next divine course.

Launch the App →
The Journey

Key Milestones

🏠

Age 17 — Taking Responsibility

Stephen begins fending for himself after his mother’s passing. His three siblings move in with him in Oke-Odo, Lagos — the four of them becoming the first pillars of NABS.

📖

The Sunday Gatherings — Alimosho LGA

40 children gather every Sunday evening around God’s Word. No structure. No funding. Just a young man and a burning conviction that the Word transforms lives.

🏅

2022 — First Lagos Bible Quiz

NABS hosts its inaugural Bible Quiz. 33% of participants are Muslim students. The overall winner is Balogun Hamdalat — a Muslim girl in SS3. The movement proves it transcends every boundary.

💻

Smallville Infotech Founded

Stephen launches Smallville Infotech to build NABS’s digital infrastructure — producing the NAIJA_NABS web application with devotionals, competitions, and leaderboards.

🏆

2024 — Bible Brains Challenge Win

Stephen Adekanmbi wins the Youth Odyssey category at the Bible Brains Challenge — ₦200,000. NABS scholars begin winning on national stages.

🌍

Finding Apollos — Semi Finals

NABS makes 50% of the Finding Apollos Semi-Finals at Lagos — sending 12 scholars to compete at the highest level.

🎖

Finding Apollos — Grand Finale Top 3

John Udeji, Favour Uchenna, and Joseph Titilayo win the Top 3 places at the Finding Apollos Grand Finale in V.I., Lagos — ₦5M+ combined.

🌟

2026 — Bible Games Champion & Next Phase

Stephen wins the 1st Edition of Bible Games with Dr. F — ₦1,000,000. The Ashoka mentorship season concludes. NAIJA_NABS enters its next divine chapter.

The Organisation

About NABS

“Not just studying but... Doing the Word” — this is not a tagline. It is a theology of action that has shaped every programme, every child, every intervention we have ever run.

📖

Deep Scripture Engagement

Rigorous, high-level textual study, doctrinal exploration, and practical application far beyond surface-level knowledge.

🏫

Secondary School Outreach

Scripture Clubs and Bible Quiz programmes across schools in Alimosho LGA, Lagos State.

✝️

Evangelism to the Unbelieving

We have consistently won unbelieving youth — including Muslims — into active relationship with God’s Word.

🎓

Scholarship & Sponsorship

We silently sponsor exceptional Bible scholars with stipends, mentorship, and support for uninterrupted formation.

🤝

Community Formation

From 40 children in a neighbourhood to an interconnected online community — we build belonging around the Word.

🌍

Faith & Education Integration

Our next-phase vision blends deep Scripture engagement with world-class formal education — scholars who solve problems and carry divine authority.

National Assembly of Bible Scholars — a movement built on the conviction that the Word of God, studied deeply and lived boldly, transforms every community it touches.

Digital Infrastructure · Smallville Infotech

The NAIJA_NABS App

A full-featured web platform for Bible scholars — daily devotionals, competitions, leaderboards, and community messaging. Built by Stephen Olabamiji through Smallville Infotech.

Launch App Now →
The Dashboard

Your Scholar Headquarters

The NABS dashboard gives every scholar a home base — daily scripture readings, live competition announcements, community leaderboards, and direct access to all platform features.

Built as a Progressive Web App, it installs on any device — phone or laptop — through a one-tap “Install App” banner. No app store required.

Access the App →

Dashboard

Daily scripture, competitions, and leaderboard — all in one view.

Daily Devotional Quiz

Read today’s scripture. Answer 3 deep questions. One attempt per day — 30 points at stake.

Ongoing Competitions

Live Bible competitions — DaWord Quiz Season 7 featured ₦100,000 prize.

How to Use NAIJA_NABS

Complete in-app guide: registration, scoring, devotionals, competition submission.

Platform Features

Everything a Bible Scholar Needs

📖

Daily Devotional

Today’s scripture with 3 deep questions. One attempt only — 10 points per correct answer.

🏅

Scholar’s Creed

This week’s pledge from the Executives. A weekly spiritual commitment to anchor every scholar.

🏆

Bible Competitions

Submit links. When approved, you earn +10 points. Approved ones appear on the dashboard.

📊

Leaderboard

Monthly and all-time community rankings. See where you stand among scholars nationwide.

💬

Scholar Messaging

Chat with any scholar across the community. Direct messages and group learning.

👥

Talk to the Excos

Direct line to NABS executives for guidance, accountability, and spiritual direction.

Getting Started

How to Join in 4 Steps

📝

Register

Create your account on the NAIJA_NABS web app. Provide your details and await admin approval.

✅

Get Approved

An executive reviews and approves your account. Access granted within a short time.

📱

Install the App

Tap the “Install App” banner. Works on phone and laptop — no store needed.

🚀

Start Studying

Open your daily devotional, join competitions, check the leaderboard, and connect with scholars.

Ready to Begin Your Scholar Journey?

Join hundreds of Bible scholars already on NAIJA_NABS. Study deeper. Compete bolder. Do the Word.

Launch NAIJA_NABS →

Competition Gallery

NABS Scholar Victories

Semi-Finals

NABS Dominates Finding Apollos — 50% of the Semi-Finals

A historic moment: 12 NABS scholars made the Finding Apollos Semi-Finals at Lagos — representing 50% of all semi-finalists. John Udeji, Ezinec Iheonunaekwu, Jeremiah Odunsanya, Joshua Obioma, Tunde Daniels, Stephen Adekanmbi, Favour Uchenna, Victor Salihu, Deborah Omowaye, Joseph Titilayo, Daniel Ogunsanya, and Victoria Akinbohun carried the NABS banner to the national stage.

Finding ApollosLagos12 Scholars
Grand Finale

NABS Sweeps Top 3 — Finding Apollos Grand Finale, V.I. Lagos

Three NABS scholars — Favour Uchenna, John Udeji, and Joseph Titilayo — claimed the Top 3 positions at the Finding Apollos Grand Finale in Victoria Island, Lagos. The community that started in Alimosho stood victorious at the highest level of national Bible competition.

Finding ApollosV.I. Lagos
₦5M+

Combined prizes — Top 3 NABS scholars

2024 · Winner

Stephen Olabamiji Wins Bible Brains Challenge — Youth Odyssey

The founder himself leads by example. Stephen Adekanmbi won the Youth Odyssey category at the Bible Brains Challenge on December 8th, 2024, receiving ₦200,000. A leader who still competes is a leader who still grows.

Bible BrainsDec 2024
₦200,000

Youth Odyssey Winner — Adekanmbi Stephen

Bible Games · 1st Ed.

Stephen Olabamiji Wins 1st Edition — Bible Games with Dr. F, Lekki

Stephen wins the inaugural edition of Bible Games with Dr. F at Lekki, Lagos — presented by Abiodun Falade Scriptural Ministries. On 22nd March 2026, he was awarded One Million Naira, confirming the founder of NABS is not just an organiser — he is an elite Bible scholar.

Bible Games Dr. FLekki · Mar 2026
₦1,000,000

Winner — Adekanmbi Stephen · 22nd March 2026

Bible Games · 3rd Ed.

Favour Uchenna Wins 3rd Edition — Bible Games with Dr. F

NABS scholar Favour Uchenna continued her winning streak by taking home One Million Naira at the 3rd Edition of Bible Games with Dr. F at Lekki, Lagos on 5th April 2026. A scholar forged by NABS, proven on the national stage.

Bible Games Dr. FLekki · Apr 2026
₦1,000,000

Winner — Favour Uchenna · 5th April 2026

Bible Games · 7th Ed.

Matthew Samuel O. Wins 7th Edition — Bible Games with Dr. F

NABS scholar Matthew Samuel O. — known as Samuel Zion — won the 7th Edition of Bible Games with Dr. F at Lekki, Lagos on 3rd May 2026, receiving ₦300,000. Three NABS scholars. Three different editions. One community, consistently raising the standard.

Bible Games Dr. FLekki · May 2026
₦300,000

Winner — Matthew Samuel O. · 3rd May 2026

Stories of Transformation

Scholars Shaped by the Word

Balogun Hamdalat

2022 Quiz Champion

A Muslim SS3 student who won the first NABS Lagos Bible Quiz — outscoring every Christian participant. Now a 400-level university student.

Stephen Mary Oizar

NABS Scholarship Recipient

Received monthly stipends from NABS for a full year. Now a 400-level Pharmacy student at University of Ilorin.

Joseph Edoh

Silently Sponsored

A Bible scholar from Pacey Manuel College, Abule Egba — sponsored by NABS when he needed someone to believe in him.

Elizabeth (Joybridge College)

Silently Sponsored

An exceptional student from Joybridge College, sponsored quietly by NABS. A testimony of what happens when talent meets support.

Favour Uchenna

Top 3 Finding Apollos · ₦1M Bible Games

Leaderboard #2. Finding Apollos Grand Finalist. Winner of the 3rd Edition of Bible Games with Dr. F. ₦1,000,000 prize.

John Udeji

Finding Apollos Grand Finalist

Top 3 at the Finding Apollos Grand Finale, Victoria Island Lagos. A testament to NABS’s scholarly formation.

Insight · Revelation · Application

Articles & Insights

Deep scripture engagement doesn’t stop at the quiz table. These articles explore the Word, the movement, and the intersection of faith and innovation — authored from the heart of NABS by Stephen Olabamiji.

Scripture · Leadership

Why the Word Must Be Done, Not Just Studied — A Theology of Action

The tagline of NABS is not decorative. It is a confrontation with the comfortable Christianity that mistakes knowledge for obedience. James 1:22 did not say “be hearers and studiers” — it said doers. This article unpacks the theology behind the entire NABS movement and why it is the only viable future for the Nigerian Church.

Stephen Olabamiji · NABS Movement · Featured
Read Article →
Innovation · Technology

Building the NAIJA_NABS App — How Faith Meets Tech in Alimosho

How a young economist with no formal software training built a full-featured web platform for Bible scholars through Smallville Infotech — with devotionals, leaderboards, and live competitions.

Stephen Olabamiji · Smallville Infotech
Read Article →
Community · Evangelism

Hamdalat Won Our Bible Quiz — And She Was Muslim. Here’s What That Means.

At the inaugural NABS Lagos Bible Quiz in 2022, 33% of participants were Muslim students. The overall winner was Balogun Hamdalat — an SS3 Muslim girl. This article reflects on what that moment revealed about the Word of God’s power to transcend every human boundary.

Stephen Olabamiji · NABS Community
Read Article →
Scholarship · Mentorship

The Quiet Philanthropy — Sponsoring Scholars Nobody Sees

Stephen Mary Oizar is now in 400-level Pharmacy at UNILORIN. Joseph Edoh of Pacey Manuel College got stipends from NABS when nobody else believed. This article is about what it means to invest in a scholar — not for credit, but for God’s glory.

Stephen Olabamiji · NABS Scholarship
Read Article →
Competition · Excellence

NABS Made 50% of Finding Apollos’ Semi-Finals — What That Number Really Means

When 12 out of the Finding Apollos semi-finalists were from the same community, something deeper than talent was at work. This article unpacks the training philosophy, competitive preparation, and the spiritual culture behind one of Nigeria’s most remarkable Bible quiz results.

NABS Editorial · Finding Apollos
Read Article →
Faith · Innovation · The 36

Two Movements, One Vision — NABS and The 36 Innovative Delegates

Stephen Olabamiji sits in a rare intersection: most ministries are not technical, and most tech founders are not spiritually intellectual. This article explores how NABS and The 36 Innovative Delegates are two expressions of one mandate — leaders who carry both divine authority and worldly competence.

Stephen Olabamiji · The 36 · Smallville Infotech
Read Article →
Scripture · Devotion

How to Study the Bible Deeply — The NABS Framework for Serious Scholars

Surface-level Bible reading produces surface-level faith. The NABS approach to scripture study — drawn from years of running competitions, clubs, and devotionals — offers a practical framework for going deeper into the Word every day.

NABS Editorial · Bible Study
Read Article →

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I join NABS? +
Register on the NAIJA_NABS web app. Once approved by an Executive, you gain full access to devotionals, competitions, messaging, and the leaderboard. You can also join the WhatsApp community for immediate connection.
Is NABS open to people of all faiths? +
Yes. At our very first Lagos Bible Quiz in 2022, 33% of participants were Muslim students — and the overall winner was a Muslim girl, Balogun Hamdalat. The Word of God is for every searching heart.
How can I sponsor a NABS scholar? +
Email nabsscholar@gmail.com with the subject “Sponsorship Inquiry.” NABS has a history of quietly sponsoring exceptional Bible scholars with stipends, mentorship, and academic support.
Who built the NABS App? +
The NAIJA_NABS web application was built by Smallville Infotech, the digital arm of NABS, founded and led by Stephen Olabamiji. It is a Progressive Web App (PWA) that works on both phones and laptops without requiring an app store download.
How can my school or church partner with NABS? +
NABS runs Scripture Clubs and Bible Quiz programmes in secondary schools and churches across Lagos. Email nabsscholar@gmail.com or send a message on WhatsApp to explore a partnership.

National Assembly of Bible Scholars — a movement built on the conviction that the Word of God, studied deeply and lived boldly, transforms every community it touches.
